hypothesis: If the Force Delta metric captures genuine physical inconsistency in the learned potential energy surface, then incorporating it as a differentiable regularization term during fine-tuning of pre-trained direct-force models will simultaneously reduce the Force Delta magnitude and improve generalization to out-of-distribution structures, with the degree of improvement correlating inversely with the size and chemical diversity of the fine-tuning dataset—such that regularization provides the greatest benefit when only sparse domain-specific data is available.
